content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter Weekly Report (Apr 26 - 2, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The primary focus for this week was ensuring the continued stability and compliance of the `plugin-jupiter` integration following the deprecation of the Jupiter lite-api. The development team successfully migrated all service requests to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint and implemented mandatory API key authentication. These updates, alongside foundational improvements to the repository's build configuration, ensure that the plugin remains functional and aligned with current Jupiter infrastructure requirements.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### API Migration and Authentication
The core objective this week was to maintain service continuity for quote and swap requests. Through PR [#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4), the team completed the following:
*   **Endpoint Migration:** Transitioned all service requests from the deprecated `lite-api.jup.ag` to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint.
*   **Authentication Implementation:** Integrated support for `JUPITER_API_KEY` via runtime settings, ensuring that all requests now correctly inject the key into the `x-api-key` header.

### Build and Repository Infrastructure
To support the project's long-term maintainability and build processes, PR [#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4) also introduced several structural enhancements:
*   **Build Configuration:** Added `build.ts` and `tsconfig.build.json` to standardize the build pipeline.
*   **Repository Hygiene:** Updated `.gitignore` and added essential project documentation to improve the overall repository structure and developer experience.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** No specific issues were formally closed during this period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** There were no new issues opened, and no active issues were reported. The project currently maintains a clean issue tracker following the successful implementation of the API migration.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Development activity this week was concentrated on the successful execution of PR [#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4). The work reflects a focused effort to align the plugin with external API requirements, ensuring that the ElizaOS ecosystem remains compatible with the latest Jupiter infrastructure. There were no reports of external contributor friction or unresolved discussions, indicating a stable and efficient development cycle for this period.
